skill.name: chatcrystal-task-recall
skill.description: Recall ChatCrystal project memories before substantial implementation, refactoring, migration, configuration, investigation, or optimization work.
skill.summary: Use ChatCrystal memory recall before non-trivial repository work.
skill.author: ZengLiangYi

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography and Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by descriptive text and Markdown code formatting. The only confirmed concern is low-risk data sharing with the optional ChatCrystal recall tool, which may receive task goals, paths, branch names, and related file names.

low_findings.title: Task Metadata Shared With Recall Tool
low_findings.description: The workflow instructs the agent to call recall_for_task with the task goal, project_dir, cwd, branch, related_files, files_touched, and source_agent when available. This is expected for memory recall, but it can disclose repository paths and task context to the MCP tool provider.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists task and repository metadata to pass into recall_for_task. The usage appears legitimate, so the risk is privacy disclosure rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md, but the flagged content is Markdown inline code and a JSON example. No executable Ruby, shell, or command invocation is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit Markdown code spans and a fenced JSON block. There is no runtime script, interpreter directive, or command execution path in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md line 3 and agents/openai.yaml line 3. Both lines are plain-language skill descriptions, and no cryptographic algorithm, hash function, key handling, or encryption logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions about recalling ChatCrystal memories. They do not mention or implement MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic primitive.

content.value_statement: Non-trivial repository work can repeat old mistakes when prior decisions are forgotten. This skill prompts Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to recall ChatCrystal memories before planning or editing.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to recall ChatCrystal memories for substantial project work.
Prioritizes project memories before global memories.
Specifies the recall_for_task input fields for implementation and investigation tasks.
Explains how to proceed when ChatCrystal Core or MCP access is unavailable.
Tells the agent to surface recall warnings without blocking normal work.
limitations: Requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access for full memory recall.
Does not provide its own memory database or storage system.
May return no useful memories for new projects or sparse history.
Can share task metadata with the configured recall tool.

use_cases.title: Prepare Repository Changes
use_cases.description: Recall previous project decisions before implementing a feature, refactor, or migration.

use_cases.title: Investigate Regressions
use_cases.description: Check prior fixes, pitfalls, and warnings before debugging a recurring issue.

use_cases.title: Keep Agent Work Consistent
use_cases.description: Give AI assistants a repeatable workflow for using stored project context before major tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Recall Before Feature Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall before implementing this feature. Recall relevant project memories, then summarize the useful context before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Recall Before Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall for this investigation. Include the current repository path, branch, and related files if available.

prompt_templates.title: Recall Before Refactoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall before refactoring this module. Prioritize project memories and note any global memories only if they change the approach.

prompt_templates.title: Recall With Fallback Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall for this migration. If recall_for_task is unavailable or returns no matches, state that briefly and continue with the work.

output_examples.input: Use prior memory before changing the notes export endpoint.
output_examples.output: The agent recalls project memories, reports relevant endpoint conventions, and applies those constraints before planning the change.

output_examples.input: Investigate this recurring build failure with project context.
output_examples.output: The agent checks ChatCrystal recall, surfaces any prior build warnings, and continues normally if no useful memory is found.

output_examples.input: Refactor the authentication module without losing prior decisions.
output_examples.output: The agent recalls project-first context, notes applicable patterns, and uses global memories only as supplemental guidance.

best_practices: Use the skill for non-trivial work with repository context.
Review recalled memories before proposing or editing code.
Avoid sending sensitive paths or file names unless needed for recall.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for trivial edits or simple conversational answers.
Do not claim memory was recalled when the tool is unavailable.
Do not install ChatCrystal Core automatically as part of the workflow.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to recall ChatCrystal memories before substantial repository work.

faq.question: Does it work without ChatCrystal Core?
faq.answer: It can still guide the workflow, but full memory recall requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access.

faq.question: Which memories are prioritized?
faq.answer: Project memories are primary. Global memories are supplemental and should only influence the task when relevant.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before implementation, refactoring, migrations, configuration changes, investigations, or optimization work.

faq.question: What information can it send to recall_for_task?
faq.answer: It may send the task goal, task kind, project path, current directory, branch, related files, and source agent.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found false positives in static analysis and one low-risk metadata sharing concern.
